baseline.patient_history: ___ with no significant PMH presents with RUQ pain since  yesterday afternoon Pain was sudden in onset and nonradiating.  He had one episode of emesis yesterday with intermittent nausea.  He reports episodes of RUQ and epigastric pain in the past,  mostly after eating heavy meals, especially eggs. He denies  fevers, chills, diarrhea, and malaise. His pain has resolved  since administration of pain medicine.     Past Medical History: PMH: Psoriasis  PSH: Right inguinal hernia repair    Social History: ___ Family History: No history of biliary or liver disease.
baseline.physical_examination: VS: 100.1 ___ 18 100% RA  Gen: Appears well, NAD CV: RRR Resp: CTAB, no distress Abd: Soft, mildly distended, no tympany, very mildly tender in RUQ, no rebound, no guarding Ext: Warm, no edema

decision_points.ordered: Ultrasound Abdomen (LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN))

visible_prior_imaging.report:
CHEST RADIOGRAPH PERFORMED ON ___:


FINDINGS:
PA and lateral views of the chest are obtained.  Lungs are clear
and well inflated.  No focal consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax. 
Cardiomediastinal silhouette is normal.  Bony structures are intact. 
Degenerative spurring in the mid thoracic spine is noted.  No free air below
the right hemidiaphragm.

ABDOMINAL RADIOGRAPH PERFORMED ON ___:


FINDINGS:
Supine and upright views of the abdomen and pelvis were provided. 
Bowel gas pattern is unremarkable without signs of ileus or obstruction. 
There is no free air below the right hemidiaphragm.  Lung bases appear clear. 
No worrisome calcifications are seen.  The bony structures appear intact
though note is made of degenerative changes of the hip joints with acetabular
spurring noted.  Also noted is a mild levoscoliosis of the lumbar spine.

decision_points.masked_result_of_this_test:
TECHNIQUE:
Transabdominal sonographic images were obtained.

FINDINGS:
There are innumerable shadowing gallstones layering dependently
within the gallbladder.  The gallbladder is not overly distended, though there
is mild wall thickening up to 5 mm with trace pericholecystic fluid.  The
common bile duct measures up to 8 mm.  In the distal common bile duct,
shadowing echogenic stones are seen consistent with choledocholithiasis.

There is no focal liver lesion.  Limited images of the right kidney are
unremarkable without masses, hydronephrosis, or stones.  Hepatopetal flow is
seen within the main portal vein.  The hepatic veins are patent.  The
visualized head and body of the pancreas appear normal.
